name: Apapacho Taqueria

notes: Apapacho's current owner-issued two-page PDF and live Square order categories reconcile to 40 food and non-alcoholic products across seven sections.

All 51 frozen rows were reconciled: 28 exact matches, four normalized matches, three reviewed variants, and sixteen stale inventory/event/alcohol/preorder rows. The 35 matched frozen presentations represent 34 current products, leaving six current omissions.

The raw 80-product Square response is inventory history, not a complete current-menu signal. Expired Valentine's, Día de Muertos, tamale preorder, Rosca de Reyes, one-night tasting, alcohol, happy-hour duplicate, and service-control SKUs were not retained solely because the API still returns them.

Apapacho publishes ingredient descriptions but no complete allergen matrix, may-contain statement, or cross-contact policy. Fourteen current products have supported positive signals and twenty-six remain accurately unavailable.

Seven matched frozen allergen outcomes were wrong: oyster mushroom was falsely treated as shellfish; corn masa was falsely treated as wheat/gluten; breaded chicken and battered shrimp omitted wheat/gluten; arroz con leche omitted milk; the historical happy-hour milanesa repeated the breading omission; and the cookie's unsupported wheat/gluten inference was removed.
Optional flour-tortilla and sunny-side-egg/meat additions are not applied to default corn-tortilla or base chilaquiles presentations, and absent terms are never treated as negative assurances.

The frozen generic extractor flattened every item into Restaurant, treated the 80-row Square inventory endpoint as current menu evidence, retained sixteen obsolete or out-of-scope rows, omitted six current products, and performed unsafe token-only allergen matching.

The reviewed parser asserts the owner-linked PDF anchors, the 80-row inventory shape, and fourteen current Square category records; uses the PDF as the in-house menu authority; accepts only six newer API-only products that are visible, fulfillable, and assigned to a live order category; and keeps beverages last.

Focused tests cover source shape, every frozen-row disposition, current omissions, historical-inventory exclusions, oyster-mushroom/corn-masa semantics, published repair invariants, target-only isolation, and idempotency.

sourceAttempts.outcome: HTTP 200. Confirmed Apapacho Taqueria, its 1280 4th Street Northeast address, owner-controlled menu route, and Square ordering infrastructure.
HTTP 200. Captured 80 owner-domain Square inventory products with visibility, fulfillment, category, description, price, and availability metadata.
notes: The response contains historical inventory and cannot establish current-menu status without the PDF and live category surface.

sourceAttempts.outcome: HTTP 200. Captured the two-page owner-issued Winter Specials menu, rendered both pages, and visually verified item-to-description layout.
notes: PDF metadata was created January 15, 2026; the restaurant currently embeds this exact file on its Menu page.

sourceAttempts.outcome: HTTP 200. Confirmed the current Menu page embeds the captured PDF and offers the restaurant's Order Online route.
HTTP 200. Captured the owner-domain order page and its fourteen live Square categories, including newer Breakfast Tacos.

Reviewed the owner-issued PDF, current order categories, and all reconciled product text. Found no complete allergen matrix, may-contain statement, shared-equipment disclosure, or item-level negative assurance.

findings.summary: The frozen extraction treated Square's 80-product inventory history as current menu evidence and retained expired or out-of-scope products.
findings.resolution: Used the embedded current PDF as in-house authority, required live category membership for six newer API-only additions, and removed sixteen frozen historical/event/alcohol/preorder rows.

findings.summary: The frozen token matcher interpreted the culinary mushroom name 'oyster mushroom' as shellfish.
findings.resolution: Added the exact oyster-mushroom exclusion before shellfish matching and removed shellfish from Tacos de Mushrooms.

findings.summary: Fried Corn Quesadilla explicitly uses corn masa, but the frozen output added wheat and gluten.
findings.resolution: Limited wheat/gluten positives to fixed flour tortilla, breaded, or battered terms; the current quesadilla retains only milk.

findings.summary: The frozen output omitted wheat/gluten from breaded chicken and battered shrimp and omitted milk from Arroz con Leche.
findings.resolution: Mapped only the fixed breaded, battered, mayo, shrimp, cheese/crema, and leche terms while leaving incomplete items unavailable.

findings.summary: Every frozen product was flattened to Restaurant and six current products were omitted.
findings.resolution: Restored seven sections and current Chilaquiles, Seasonal Popsicle, Bottled Water, Mexican Coke, Diet Coke, and Sangria Señorial.

repairs.summary: Rebuilt Apapacho's current food/non-alcoholic catalog and corrected stale inventory, structure, omissions, allergen semantics, and evidence boundaries.
